skill.name: flow-nexus-platform
skill.description: Manage Flow Nexus platform workflows for authentication, sandbox execution, deployments, payments, challenges, storage, and real-time monitoring.
skill.summary: Operate Flow Nexus accounts, sandboxes, deployments, billing, challenges, storage, and monitoring from AI coding tools.
skill.author: Flow Nexus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, network, environment access, and weak cryptography patterns. Most command and cryptography hits are false positives from Markdown JavaScript examples, but the skill does document legitimate high-impact platform actions including sandbox code execution, package installation, secret handling, deployments, storage, and payments. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, so publication is acceptable with a clear warning.

medium_findings.title: Remote Sandbox Code and Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill documents creating and configuring sandboxes with startup scripts, package installation, run commands, and arbitrary code execution. This is expected for the platform, but misuse could run untrusted code or deploy unsafe workloads.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly include sandbox_create, sandbox_configure, sandbox_execute, install_packages, run_commands, and startup_script. The behavior is documented platform functionality, not hidden malicious code.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Environment Variable Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The examples pass API keys, database URLs, NextAuth secrets, Redis URLs, and an Anthropic key into platform operations. These are placeholders, but the workflow requires careful secret handling by users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file shows concrete env_vars and deployment variable fields for credentials and connection strings. The values are examples, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Payment and Auto-Refill Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill documents creating payment links and enabling automatic credit refills. These actions can affect user billing and should require explicit user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The payment examples are direct platform operations, including create_payment_link and configure_auto_refill. No abuse is shown, but the financial impact is clear.

medium_findings.title: Storage and Execution File Access
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill documents uploading, listing, deleting, and retrieving generated files. These operations can expose or modify user data if used with the wrong bucket, path, or execution stream.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file storage and execution file examples are explicit and broad. The risk depends on platform authorization and user-supplied paths.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fence External Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Many static external command alerts are triggered by JavaScript examples inside Markdown code fences. The skill file itself is documentation and does not contain Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced JavaScript examples of MCP tool calls, not executable Ruby or shell backticks. No local command invocation code is present in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alerts align with words such as JWT, OAuth2, authentication, and security guidance. No MD5, SHA-1, or custom cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced context discusses authentication app metadata, JWT, and OAuth2 tags. It does not implement or recommend a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Support URLs
low_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill lists Flow Nexus documentation, API, status, community, GitHub, Discord, and example repository URLs. These links are user-facing references rather than hidden network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs are visible in the documentation and one advanced sandbox example clones a placeholder repository. They are not concealed exfiltration endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sandbox Startup Scripts and Run Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents startup_script and run_commands fields that can install packages, run migrations, seed data, and execute shell-style setup steps inside a sandbox.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly show command strings in sandbox configuration. The commands are examples, but the capability is real.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Capable Sandbox Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The sandbox_execute example fetches data from an external HTTPS endpoint. This is normal for development, but it could be used to move data outside the platform.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example contains a direct fetch call in code submitted for sandbox execution. No credential exfiltration is shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secrets Passed to Platform Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows credentials and connection strings being supplied through env_vars, deployment variables, and an anthropic_key field.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The secret-related fields are clear in the examples. The shown values are placeholders, so there is no evidence of leaked real credentials.

content.value_statement: Flow Nexus work spans accounts, sandboxes, deployments, credits, challenges, storage, and monitoring. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a single guide for operating those workflows through Flow Nexus tools.
actual_capabilities: Guides registration, login, logout, password reset, email verification, profile updates, and user statistics.
Documents creating, configuring, executing, listing, stopping, deleting, and inspecting Flow Nexus sandboxes.
Covers app search, template listing, app publishing, app updates, deployment, analytics, and installed app views.
Explains credit balances, transaction history, payment links, auto-refill settings, and earning credits.
Includes challenge discovery, solution submission, completion tracking, leaderboards, and achievements.
Describes file storage, real-time subscriptions, execution streams, audit logs, health checks, and auth initialization.
limitations: The skill is documentation and requires the Flow Nexus MCP tools to perform actions.
Users need valid Flow Nexus credentials, permissions, and billing access for many workflows.
It does not validate user code, deployment source, or challenge solutions before submission.
Examples use placeholders, so users must provide secure real values in their own environment.

use_cases.title: Launch a Development Sandbox
use_cases.description: Create a Node, Python, React, Next.js, vanilla, base, or Claude Code sandbox and run project setup steps.

use_cases.title: Publish and Monitor Platform Apps
use_cases.description: Search templates, publish applications, deploy starter projects, review analytics, and inspect installed apps.

use_cases.title: Manage Credits and Challenges
use_cases.description: Track balances, review payments, configure auto-refill, browse challenges, submit solutions, and monitor leaderboard progress.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Account
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Flow Nexus authentication status, profile, credit balance, and recent payment history. Ask before changing any settings.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Sandbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Flow Nexus development sandbox for a Node API. Include required packages, safe environment variables, and a short startup plan.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Find a suitable backend template, explain the deployment variables I need, then deploy it only after I confirm the configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Platform Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Flow Nexus sandboxes, deployments, storage, execution streams, credits, and audit logs. Identify cleanup, cost, and security actions.

output_examples.input: User asks to create a development sandbox for an Express API.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies a Node sandbox template, confirms package and environment requirements, creates the sandbox, and reports the sandbox identifier and next steps.

output_examples.input: User asks to deploy a backend template with database settings.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists required variables, warns about secrets, requests confirmation, deploys the template after approval, and summarizes deployment status.

output_examples.input: User asks to reduce Flow Nexus costs.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews balances, sandboxes, storage, and auto-refill settings, then recommends cleanup and monitoring actions without making billing changes automatically.

best_practices: Confirm before running sandbox code, changing billing settings, publishing apps, or deleting storage.
Use placeholders in prompts and provide secrets only through approved secure Flow Nexus fields.
Clean up unused sandboxes, temporary files, and old execution streams to reduce cost and exposure.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production secrets into casual chat or reusable prompt templates.
Do not enable auto-refill or purchase credits without explicit user approval.
Do not run untrusted startup scripts, package installs, or challenge solutions without reviewing their behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill run code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It documents Flow Nexus tool calls. Actual execution requires available MCP tools and user authorization.

faq.question: Can it manage Flow Nexus billing?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers credit balances, payment history, payment links, and auto-refill settings.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production deployments?
faq.answer: It can guide deployments, but users should review code, secrets, permissions, and costs before production use.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends environment variables, private buckets, audit log review, and careful resource cleanup.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill can guide code execution, deployments, storage changes, secret handling, and payment operations.
